title: 💻 Code Special — all lab code types
blurb: Pure-code practice covering every lab code module as rotating fill-in-the-blank: Keras model building (MLP/CNN/RNN), compile/fit/evaluate, initialization, regularization, hyperparameter search, classical ML (sklearn), and the PyTorch/others (GNN, PINN, DQN, RAG). Type the missing tokens; each visit rotates a variant.

knowledge_points.title: Keras core (build / compile / fit / evaluate)
knowledge_points.recap: The Keras workflow: build a Sequential model of Dense layers (first layer needs input_shape, output layer's units = #classes), pick the output activation/loss pairing (softmax+categorical/sparse_categorical for multi-class, sigmoid+binary_crossentropy for binary), then compile(optimizer, loss, metrics) -> fit(..., validation_data=...) -> evaluate on the test set. Preprocess with train_test_split, reshape + /255 normalization, and to_categorical for one-hot labels.

knowledge_points.title: CNN & RNN (Keras)
knowledge_points.recap: CNNs stack Conv2D + MaxPooling2D (optionally BatchNormalization) then Flatten -> Dense -> softmax; use sparse_categorical_crossentropy when labels are integers, categorical_crossentropy when one-hot. Sequence models use Embedding then a recurrent layer (SimpleRNN/LSTM) — set return_sequences=True on every layer that feeds another recurrent layer, and leave it default (False) on the last. A lightweight text alternative is Embedding -> Conv1D -> GlobalMaxPooling1D. Binary sentiment heads use Dense(1,'sigmoid') with binary_crossentropy (often the rmsprop optimizer).

questions.q: **Fill in the blanks** to build a basic CNN classifier (rotating variant):
questions.answer:
```python
model = Sequential()
model.add(Conv2D(32, (3, 3), activation='relu', input_shape=(28, 28, 1)))
model.add(MaxPooling2D((2, 2)))
model.add(Flatten())
model.add(Dense(64, activation='relu'))
model.add(Dense(10, activation='softmax'))
```

knowledge_points.title: Initialization, regularization, tuning
knowledge_points.recap: Weight init scales the random weights by fan-in: Xavier/Glorot uses 1/sqrt(n) (good for tanh/sigmoid), He uses 1/sqrt(n/2) (good for ReLU). Fight overfitting with kernel_regularizer=L2(0.01), Dropout(0.5), and EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', patience=..., restore_best_weights=True) passed via callbacks. Tune hyperparameters by wrapping the model in KerasClassifier(build_fn=...) and searching a param_grid with GridSearchCV.

questions.q: **Fill in the blanks** for weight initialization scaling inside the layer loop (rotating variant):
questions.answer:
```python
for n in layer_sizes:
    # Xavier / Glorot: good for tanh/sigmoid
    W_xavier = np.random.randn(n, n) * (1 / np.sqrt(n))
    # He: good for ReLU
    W_he = np.random.randn(n, n) * (1 / np.sqrt(n / 2))
```

variants.code:
```python
for n in layer_sizes:
    W_xavier = np.random.randn(n, n) * (1 / np.sqrt(____(1)))
    W_he     = np.random.randn(n, n) * (1 / np.sqrt(n / ____(2)))
```

```python
for n in layer_sizes:
    W_xavier = np.random.____(1)(n, n) * (1 / np.____(2)(n))
    W_he     = np.random.randn(n, n) * (1 / np.sqrt(n / 2))
```

knowledge_points.title: Classical ML (sklearn)
knowledge_points.recap: Distance-based models (KNN, SVM) need feature scaling: StandardScaler.fit_transform on train, .transform on test. KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=k).fit/.predict; pick K by looping and tracking np.mean(pred != y_test). Tune SVC over C/gamma/kernel with GridSearchCV(..., refit=True, cv=5). Tree models DecisionTreeClassifier(max_depth=...) and RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=...) don't require scaling.

questions.q: **Fill in the blanks** for the scaled KNN pipeline and choose-K loop (rotating variant):
questions.answer:
```python
scaler = StandardScaler()
X_train = scaler.fit_transform(X_train)
X_test  = scaler.transform(X_test)

knn = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=5)
knn.fit(X_train, y_train)
pred = knn.predict(X_test)

error = []
for k in range(1, 20):
    knn = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=k)
    knn.fit(X_train, y_train)
    pred = knn.predict(X_test)
    error.append(np.mean(pred != y_test))
```

variants.code:
```python
scaler = StandardScaler()
X_train = scaler.____(1)(X_train)   # learn stats + apply
X_test  = scaler.____(2)(X_test)    # apply only
knn = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=5)
knn.fit(X_train, y_train)
```

```python
for k in range(1, 20):
    knn = ____(1)(n_neighbors=k)
    knn.fit(X_train, y_train)
    pred = knn.predict(X_test)
    error.append(np.mean(pred ____(2) y_test))
```

knowledge_points.title: PyTorch & others (GNN / PINN / DQN / RAG)
knowledge_points.recap: These labs use PyTorch, not Keras. A GNN subclasses torch.nn.Module, stacks GCNConv layers, and forward(x, edge_index) applies F.relu between them. The PyTorch training loop is optimizer.zero_grad() -> forward -> loss -> loss.backward() -> optimizer.step(). A PINN gets derivatives with torch.autograd.grad(..., create_graph=True) and minimizes physics_loss + boundary_loss. A DQN forms the Bellman target reward + gamma * np.amax(Q(next_state)).

questions.q: **Fill in the blanks** for the PyTorch-Geometric GCN and its training loop (rotating variant, PyTorch not Keras):
questions.answer:
```python
class GCN(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv1 = GCNConv(num_features, 16)
        self.conv2 = GCNConv(16, num_classes)
    def forward(self, x, edge_index):
        x = self.conv1(x, edge_index)
        x = F.relu(x)
        x = self.conv2(x, edge_index)
        return F.log_softmax(x, dim=1)

# training loop
for epoch in range(200):
    optimizer.zero_grad()
    out = model(data.x, data.edge_index)
    loss = criterion(out[data.train_mask], data.y[data.train_mask])
    loss.backward()
    optimizer.step()
```

variants.code:
```python
self.conv1 = ____(1)(num_features, 16)
self.conv2 = ____(1)(16, num_classes)
def forward(self, x, edge_index):
    x = self.conv1(x, edge_index)
    x = F.____(2)(x)
    x = self.conv2(x, edge_index)
    return F.log_softmax(x, dim=1)
```

```python
for epoch in range(200):
    optimizer.____(1)()          # clear old gradients
    out = model(data.x, data.edge_index)
    loss = criterion(out[data.train_mask], data.y[data.train_mask])
    loss.____(2)()               # backprop
    optimizer.____(3)()          # update weights
```

questions.q: **Fill in the blanks** for the PINN autodiff residual and the DQN Bellman target (rotating variant):
questions.answer:
```python
# PINN: derivatives via autograd
u_x  = torch.autograd.grad(u, x, grad_outputs=torch.ones_like(u), create_graph=True)[0]
u_xx = torch.autograd.grad(u_x, x, grad_outputs=torch.ones_like(u_x), create_graph=True)[0]
physics_loss  = torch.mean((u_xx - f) ** 2)
boundary_loss = torch.mean((u_bc - u_true_bc) ** 2)
loss = physics_loss + boundary_loss

# DQN: Bellman target
target = reward + gamma * np.amax(model.predict(next_state)[0])
```

```python
u_xx = torch.autograd.____(1)(u_x, x, grad_outputs=torch.ones_like(u_x), create_graph=True)[0]
physics_loss = torch.mean((u_xx - f) ____(2) 2)
```

```python
target = reward + ____(1) * np.____(2)(model.predict(next_state)[0])
```
